questions.q: The resistance of a conductivity cell is $\mathrm{X} \Omega$ when 0.01 M HA is present in the cell. The pH of the cell is 3 , the limiting molar conductivity of HA at infinite dilution is $400 \mathrm{~S} \mathrm{~cm}^2 \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$. Find the value of ' X ' if the value of the cell constant is $0.01 \mathrm{~cm}^{-1}$.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The emf of the cell, $\mathrm{M}(\mathrm{s}) \mid \mathrm{M}_n X_n(0.01 \mathrm{M}) \| \mathrm{M}_n X_n(0.1 \mathrm{M}) / \mathrm{M}(\mathrm{s})$ is found to be 0.0295 V at $25^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$. If $\mathrm{M}_n X_n$ is a soluble salt, find the value of $n$.
questions.solution:
$\mathrm{nM} \longrightarrow \mathrm{M}_{\mathrm{n}_{\mathrm{n}}}^{\mathrm{n}^{+}}+\mathrm{ne}^{-} \quad$ (at anode)
$\frac{\mathrm{M}_{n_e^{+}}^{+}+\mathrm{ne}^{-} \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{M} \quad \text { (at cathode) }}{\text { Net all reaction: } \mathrm{M}_{n_E}^{c^{+}} \longrightarrow \mathrm{M}_{n_A}^{c_A^*}}$
$\therefore 0.0295=\frac{0.059}{n} \log \frac{M_{n=t}^{\left.m_n\right]}}{\left.M_{n a x}^m\right]}$
$\therefore \mathrm{n}=2$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q:
A student has 2 containers and each container has 1 L solution. He tries to form an electrochemical cell that is $\mathrm{Pt}\left|\mathrm{H}_2(1 \mathrm{~atm})\right| \mathrm{HA}\left(10^{-4} \mathrm{M}\right)\left|\left|\mathrm{H}^{+}(0.1 \mathrm{M})\right| \mathrm{H}_2(1 \mathrm{~atm})\right| \mathrm{Pt}$

If E.M.F of cell is 0.24 and pKa of HA is $X$. The value of $X$ is $\left[\frac{2.303 R T}{F}=0.06\right]$

questions.solution:
Number of moles of hypo
$\begin{aligned}
& =\frac{M V}{1000} \\
& =\frac{0.1 \times 40}{1000}=4 \times 10^{-3}
\end{aligned}$
$\therefore \quad$ Number of moles of $\mathrm{I}_2=2 \times 10^{-3}$
Mass of $\mathrm{I}_2=2 \times 10^{-3} \times 254 \mathrm{~g}$
$\begin{array}{r}
\mathrm{w}=\frac{i \times \mathrm{t} \times \mathrm{E}}{96500} \\
2 \times 10^{-3} \times 254=\frac{i \times 9650 \times 127}{96500} \\
i=0.04 \mathrm{ampere} \\
\text { ency } \quad=\frac{0.04}{0.50} \times 100=8 \%
\end{array}$

questions.q:
Consider the following cell reaction :
$2 \mathrm{AgCl}(\mathrm{~s})+\mathrm{H}_2(\mathrm{~g}) \longrightarrow \mathrm{Ag}(\mathrm{~s})+2 \mathrm{H}^{+}(\mathrm{aq})+2 \mathrm{Cl}^{-}(\mathrm{aq}) \quad \mathrm{E}^{\circ}=0.19 \mathrm{~V}$

At $\left[\mathrm{Cl}^{-}\right]=10^{-3} \mathrm{M}$ and $\mathrm{P}\left(\mathrm{H}_2\right)=0.01 \mathrm{~atm}$, find the pH , if the cell potential is 0.43 V at $25^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$.
(Take $\frac{2.303 R(298)}{F}=0.06$ )
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
A Galvanic cell is constructed with
$\mathrm{Ag}^{+} \mid \mathrm{Ag}$ and $\mathrm{Fe}^{3+} \mid \mathrm{Fe}^{2+}$ electrodes. Find the concentration of $\mathrm{Ag}^{+}$at which the emf of the cell is zero at equimolar concentrations of $\mathrm{Fe}^{2+}$ and $\mathrm{Fe}^{3+}$.
$\left(E_{\mathrm{Ag}^{\prime} \mid \mathrm{Ag}}^{\circ}=0.80 \mathrm{~V} \text { and } \mathrm{E}_{\mathrm{Fe}^{3+1} \mid \mathrm{Fe}^{2+}}^{\circ}=0.77 \mathrm{~V}\right)$ (mark the answer in M)
questions.solution:
As $\mathrm{E}^{\circ}$ for silver electrode is more positive, the cell may be represented as
$\begin{aligned}
& \mathrm{Fe}^{2+}, \mathrm{Fe}^{3+} \| \mathrm{Ag}^{+}, \mathrm{Ag}_1 \quad \mathrm{E}_{\text {cell }}^{\circ}=0.80-0.77=0.03 \mathrm{~V} \\
& \mathrm{E}_{\text {cell }}=0.03-\frac{0.0591}{1} \log \frac{\left[\mathrm{Fe}^{3+}\right]}{\left[\mathrm{Fe}^{2+}\right]\left[\mathrm{Ag}^{+}\right]} \\
& \therefore \mathrm{E}_{\text {cell }}=0.03+\frac{0.0591}{1} \log \left[\mathrm{Ag}^{+}\right] \because\left[\mathrm{Fe}^{3+}\right]=\left[\mathrm{Fe}^{2+}\right] \\
& \therefore 0=0.03+\frac{0.0591}{1} \log \left[\mathrm{Ag}^{+}\right] \quad \therefore\left[\mathrm{Ag}^{+}\right]=0.3121 \mathrm{M}
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The e.m.f of cell $\mathrm{Zn}_{(\mathrm{s})}\left|\mathrm{ZnSO}_4 \| \mathrm{CuSO}_4\right| \mathrm{Cu}_{(\mathrm{s})}$ at $25^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ is 0.03 V and temperature coefficient of e.m. f is $\left(-1.4 \times 10^{-4} \mathrm{~V}\right)$ per degree. Calculate the heat of reaction for the change taking place inside the cell. (mark the answer in $\mathrm{KJ} / \mathrm{mol}$)
questions.solution:
$F=96500 \mathrm{C}$
$\mathrm{E}=0.03 \mathrm{~V}$
$\mathrm{T}=273+25=298 \mathrm{~K}$
$\mathrm{n}=2$
$\left(\frac{\mathrm{dE}}{\mathrm{dT}}\right)_{\mathrm{p}}=-1.4 \times 10^{-4} \mathrm{v}$. per degree
As $\Delta H=n F\left[T\left(\frac{d E}{d T}\right)_{\mathrm{P}}-\mathrm{E}\right]$
$=2 \times 96500\left[298 \times\left(-1.4 \times 10^{-4}\right)-0.03\right]$
$=-13842 \mathrm{~J} / \mathrm{mol}=-13.842 \mathrm{KJ} / \mathrm{mol}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Electrolysis of aq. Solution of $\mathrm{CH}_3 \mathrm{COOK}$ is carried out. If 2 amp current is passed for 402 minutes, what volume (at STP) of gases could be produced if current efficiency is $80 \%$ (assume no absorption of gases)
questions.solution:
$$
\begin{aligned}
& 2 \mathrm{CH}_3 \mathrm{COOK} \xrightarrow{\text { evectroyss }} \mathrm{C}_2 \mathrm{H}_6+2 \mathrm{CO}_2+\mathrm{H}_2+2 \mathrm{KOH}+2 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O} \\
& \mathrm{Q}=2 \times 402 \times 60 \\
& \text { moles of } \mathrm{e}^{-}=0.5 \text {; used mole of } \mathrm{e}^{-}=\frac{0.5 \times 80}{100}=0.4 \\
& \text { as } 2 \text { mole } \mathrm{e}^{-} \longrightarrow 4 \text { mole gases } \\
& \text { so } 0.4 \mathrm{~mole}^{-} \longrightarrow \frac{0.4 \times 4}{2}=0.8 \text { mole gases } \\
& \mathrm{V}=0.8 \times 22.4 \mathrm{Lt}=17.92 \mathrm{Lt} .
\end{aligned}
$$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Consider the reaction, $2 \mathrm{Ag}^{+}+\mathrm{Cd} \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{Ag}+\mathrm{Cd}^{2+}$
The standard electrode potentials for $\mathrm{Ag}^{+} \longrightarrow \mathrm{Ag}$ and $\mathrm{Cd}^{2+} \longrightarrow \mathrm{Cd}$ couples are 0.80 volt and 0.40 volt respectively.
(i) What is the standard potential $\mathrm{E}^0$ for this reaction?
(ii) For the electrochemical cell, in which this reaction takes place which electrode is negative electrode? (mark the answer in volt)
questions.solution:
(i) The half reactions are:
$\begin{gathered}
\text { at cathode } \\
2 \mathrm{Ag}^{+}(\mathrm{aq})+2 \mathrm{e}^{-} \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{Ag}(\mathrm{~s})
\end{gathered}$
$\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{E}_{\mathrm{Ag} / \mathrm{Ag}}^{\circ}=0.80 \text { volt } \quad \text { (Reduction potential) } \\ & \begin{array}{l}\text { atanode } \\ \mathrm{Cd}(\mathrm{s})\end{array} \mathrm{Cd}^{2+}(\mathrm{aq})+2 \mathrm{e}^{-}\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned}
& \mathrm{E}_{\mathrm{Cd}^{+} / \mathrm{Cd}}^{\circ}=-0.40 \text { volt (Reduction potential) } \\
& \text { or } \quad E_{\mathrm{Cd}_1 / \mathrm{d}^{2+}}^{\circ}=+0.40 \text { volt } \\
& E^{\circ}=E_{{\mathrm{Cd} / C d^{+}}^{\circ}}^{\circ}+E_{\mathrm{Ag}^{+} / \mathrm{Ag}}^{\circ}=0.40+0.80=1.20 \text { volt }
\end{aligned}$
(ii) The negative electrode is always the electrode whose reduction potential has smaller value or the electrode where oxidation occurs. Thus, Cd electrode is the negative electrode.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: $1000 \mathrm{ml}, 0.5$ molar $\mathrm{CuSO}_4$ solution is electrolysed by using current of 9.65 A for 1000 second. Find the pH of solution at the end of electrolysis.
questions.solution:
Reaction at anode
$\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O} \rightarrow \frac{1}{2} \mathrm{O}_2+2 \mathrm{H}^{+}+2 \mathrm{e}^{-}$

2 mole electron produce $=2 \mathrm{~mol} \mathrm{H}^{+}$
$\frac{9.65 \times 1000}{96500} \text { produce }=0.1 \mathrm{~mol} \mathrm{H}^{+}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Consider the following at $25^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$
$\mathrm{A}_{(\mathrm{s})}\left|\mathrm{A}_{10^{-1} \mathrm{M}}^{+\mathrm{n}} \| \mathrm{H}_{10^{-2} \mathrm{M}}^{+}\right| \mathrm{H}_2 \mid \mathrm{Pt}$
The e.m.f of cell is 0.89 V and $E_{\mathrm{A} \mid \mathrm{A}^{+n}}^{\circ}$ is +0.98 V . The value of ' $n$ ' is $\left[\right.$ assume $\left.\frac{2.303 \mathrm{RT}}{\mathrm{F}}=0.06\right]$
questions.solution:
Cathode
$\mathrm{nH}^{+}+\mathrm{ne}^{-} \rightarrow \frac{\mathrm{n}}{2} \mathrm{H}_2$

Anode
$\begin{aligned}
& A(s) \rightarrow A^{+n}+n e^{-} \\
& Q=\frac{\left(H_2\right)\left(A^{+n}\right)}{\left[H^{+}\right]^n}=\frac{\left(10^{-1}\right)}{\left(10^{-2}\right)^n}=10^{2 n-1} \\
& E=E_{\text {cell }}^{\circ}-\frac{0.06}{n} \log 10^{2 n-1} \\
& \Rightarrow 0.89=0.8-\frac{0.06}{n} \log 10^{2 n-1} \\
& \Rightarrow 0.09=-\frac{0.06}{n}(2 n-1) \\
& \Rightarrow 0.09 n=-0.06(2 n-1) \\
& \Rightarrow 9 n=6(2 n-1) \\
& \Rightarrow 3 n=4 n-2 \\
& n=2
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
